_comment: Baseline parity tolerances per module. Maximum allowed difference between Dart implementation and C++ Framework golden reference. Updated whenever parity is verified. Use tool/verify_parity.dart to regenerate.
_workflow: 4. If improvements: update baseline tolerances downward
5. If regressions: tolerances exceeded => fix the bug
_format: Each module records max absolute difference observed and counts of samples exceeding standard thresholds.

_categories.exact: Modules that achieve bit-exact 0 parity. Any drift = bug.
_categories.near_exact: Modules with <1e-9 drift, limited only by JSON %.10g rounding.
_categories.transcendental_limited: Modules using sin/cos/atan2/sqrt/cbrt. Inherent ~1e-8 limit because Dart's math functions are double-precision while C++ uses float versions.
_categories.iterative: Multi-frame simulations where rounding errors accumulate.

math.easingSine.notes: Float32 cast at each step. Residual ~3e-8 from cosf vs cos precision.

math.cardanoAlgorithmForBezier.notes: Float32 cast throughout. Residual ~1e-8 from sqrt/cbrt/acos/cos in double.

math.matrixMultiply.notes: Float32List storage truncates each result. Bit-exact match.

math.matrixInverse.notes: Float32 cast at each binop. Residual <1e-9 from JSON %.10g rounding.

math.directionToRadian.notes: Float32 cast at each step. Residual <1e-9 from atan2f vs atan2 cast.

breath.sineWave.notes: Float32 cast each iteration. Residual ~3e-8 from sinf vs sin precision.

look.formula.notes: Pure arithmetic with Float32 casts. Residual <2e-9 from JSON rounding.

expression.weightSamples.notes: Writes through float32 FFI model parameters — bit-exact.

motion.curves.notes: BezierEvaluate (linear-t) accumulated drift. Max observed 9.6e-4. Could be reduced by adding Float32.cast throughout CubismMotion (currently not done due to invasiveness).

motionQueue.priorityTransition.notes: Priority motion transition with fade crossfade. Max observed 6.5e-5.

pose.120frame.notes: Writes through float32 FFI model parts — bit-exact.
